title: Government of Canada - Consultations - Consultations - All  - Open Government Portal

## Government of Canada - Consultations - Consultations - All
- Download CSV TSV JSON XML
This resource view is not available at the moment. Click here for more information.
Your browser does not support iframes.
This field is populated by the user. It is a unique reference number given to each line item in the spreadsheet. Having a unique identifier for each item will allow users locate a specific item in the registry should they need to modify or delete. The Registration Number should respect the following format: C-XXXXXXX.
Free text (comma separated list of codes from reference table)
This field identifies the name(s) of the departments who are collaborating on the identified engagement process. Please provide the numerical code associated to each department and separated by commas.

Natural Ressources Canada (NRCan)
Department of Justice Canada (JUS)

Environment and Climate Change Canada (ECCC)
Free text (comma separated list of codes from reference table)
This field indicates the different subjects related to the engagement process.
Advertising / Marketing
Copyright / Trademarks / Patents
Economic development
Exporting / Importing
Financial assistance and entitlements
Gender Based Analysis
Government procurement
Persons with disabilities
Rural and remote services
Science and technology
Training and careers
This field identifies the complete name of the consultation in English.
This field identifies the complete name of the consultation in French.
Free text (separate paragraphs with two blank lines)
This field provides information regarding the nature of consultation and engagement activities in English. This includes a description of the main objectives and expected outcomes of the engagement process. Users are invited to explain how the engagement activities, methods and/or tools will help meet the consultation’s objectives.
Free text (separate paragraphs with two blank lines)
This field provides information regarding the nature of consultation and engagement activities in French. This includes a description of the main objectives and expected outcomes of the engagement process. Users are invited to explain how the engagement activities, methods and/or tools will help meet the consultation’s objectives.
Free text (comma separated list of codes from reference table)
This field provides information regarding the target participants and audience of the engagement process.
Arts and Culture Institutions
Agriculture Production
Business and Industry Associations
Canadians Living Abroad
Construction Companies
Colleges and Universities

Date (Please format the date as YYYY-MM-DD)
This field indicates when a planned consultation will begin to accept input from participants.
Date (Please format the date as YYYY-MM-DD)
This field indicates the end date of the consultation. The consultation is considered closed when no more engagement activities will take place and/or when a department begins analysing the input received during the engagement process.
The field indicates the current status of the engagement process.
Closed – Analyzing Input
Closed – No Report Expected
Closed – Report Available
Open – Accepting Input
This field provides the link to the English engagement information profile page.
This field provides the link to the French engagement information profile page.
This field indicates if the “What we Heard” report is completed, published and made available online on the Consulting with Canadians website.
This field provides the link to the “What we Heard” report when it is available online.
This field provides the link to the “What we Heard” report when it is available online.
This field indicates whether the consultation or public engagement initiative is considered high profile. The consultation is considered high profile if it meets one or more of the criteria in the ‘Rationale’ field.
The field indicates the rationale that prompted the public engagement initiative. This field includes only criteria that define high profile public engagement.
International Commitment
Minister and/or Parliamentary Secretary Involvement and/or announcement
Parliament Committee
Speech of the Throne
